purpose: Quantization demonstration (ex05 section 5.3) — same primary model (Phi-3-medium) via Ollama's default Q4_0 GGUF quantization, for direct comparison against AirLLM and the failed oversized baseline.
prompt: Explain in one short sentence what virtual memory is.
generated_text: Virtual memory allows a computer to compensate for limited physical memory by temporarily transferring data from RAM to disk storage, creating an illusion of a
measurement_note: peak_ram_mb correctly measures the 'llama-server.exe' OS process spawned by Ollama to run the model, NOT our own Python client process. Two earlier runs (results not kept) wrongly reported ~36MB because BaseMetricsCollectorMixin originally always sampled the calling process's own RSS -- a real bug found and fixed in src/local_llm_bench/shared/metrics.py (added process_name_filter support) after manually observing llama-server.exe using ~3.6-3.76GB RAM via Task Manager / tasklist during a parallel-monitored run.
bugs_found_and_fixed_along_the_way: config ollama_tag was being derived from the Hugging Face repo name ('phi-3-medium-4k-instruct'), which is not a valid Ollama tag; added explicit 'ollama_tag': 'phi3:medium' to config/setup.json
QuantizationService used subprocess.run(['ollama','pull',...]) which failed with [WinError 2] because 'ollama' wasn't on the calling process's PATH; switched to Ollama's HTTP POST /api/pull endpoint
BaseMetricsCollectorMixin always sampled its own process's RAM; added external process-name-based sampling for QuantizationService since Ollama runs the model in a separate OS process
